name: Old-Fashioned Sour Cream Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Grease two cookie sheets.
3. In a bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and nutmeg.
4. In a separate bowl, beat together the butter and 1 cup of sugar until light and fluffy.
5. Add the egg and vanilla to the butter mixture and beat until combined.
6. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture, alternating with the sour cream, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined.
7. Divide the dough into two portions.
8. On a floured surface, roll one portion of dough into a rectangle about 1/4-inch thick.
9. Cut out cookies using a 2-inch cookie cutter.
10. Place the cookies 2 inches apart on the prepared cookie sheets.
11. Sprinkle the tops of the cookies with additional sugar.
12. Bake for 8-10 minutes, or until lightly browned.
13. Repeat with the remaining dough.
14. Decorate as desired.
15. Makes approximately 5 dozen cookies.
